assertions.text: Vehicle 1 was heading south in lane two of a two-lane protected turn lane.
The protected turn lane (with Vehicle 1) was about to merge into a southbound three-lane one-way road.
Vehicle 2 was traveling east in lane one of a nine-lane, two-way road.
Vehicle 2 turned right to travel south into lane two of the southbound three-lane one-way road.
Vehicle 1 made a right turn across a painted gore before the merge, intending to enter a parking lot.
Vehicle 1's right side and Vehicle 2's front end made contact.
There was a single contact between Vehicle 1 and Vehicle 2.
Both vehicles came to rest on the road after the contact.
Vehicle 1 was in the protected turn lane and made the right turn across the painted gore before the merge, at which point the contact with Vehicle 2 occurred.

verdicts.evidence: The available telemetry is from V2 only and contains no absolute lane or heading labels for Vehicle 1. The V2 channels show no direct measurement of another vehicle’s heading or lane position, so lane and heading claims about Vehicle 1 cannot be determined from these sensors.

Road geometry and lane-counting are not recorded in V2’s telemetry; no GPS track or map-matched lane data are present in the provided channels, so the described merge configuration cannot be confirmed from the device data.

V2’s telemetry provides V2 speed and steering but no absolute travel heading or lane index for V2; while pre-crash speed samples show V2 moving from [6.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s] to [28.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T3), the cardinal direction "east" and lane number are not available.

Steering channel shows a large steering change (raw steering from [220.0 deg at t=-5.0 s] to [17.5 deg at t=-0.5 s] and [22.5 deg at t=0.0 s](T3)), indicating a substantial steering maneuver, but steering alone does not provide absolute heading or target lane number; therefore the specific claim of turning right into southbound lane two is not verifiable from V2 telemetry.
Intent and exact path of Vehicle 1 (across a painted gore into a parking lot) are spatial facts about the other vehicle and environment that are not present in V2’s sensor channels.

V2’s crash pulse shows a dominant longitudinal delta-V of [−17.0 km/h](T2) (indicating V2 experienced a forward-directed deceleration) and a smaller lateral cumulative delta-V with peak [4.0 km/h at t=0.07 s] and final lateral [2.0 km/h](T2). These readings are consistent with a frontal impact combined with some lateral impulse, but device-frame axes and which vehicle side contacted which part cannot be determined solely from V2’s accelerometer/EDR summary without extra information on mounting orientation and positions of contact. Thus the specific statement about Vehicle 1’s right side contacting V2’s front end is not verifiable.

The CISS delta-V crash pulse is a single coherent event with cumulative longitudinal delta-V reaching a single peak of [−17.0 km/h at t=0.19 s](T2) and no separate distinct crash pulses reported; the EDR summary shows one crash pulse (single max delta-V reported)(T1)(T2), which supports a single contact event recorded by V2.

V2’s telemetry ends at impact with final cumulative delta-V and pre/post speeds for V2 only; V2’s last pre-impact speed was [28.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T3) and the EDR shows a crash pulse of [−17.0 km/h](T2), but there is no post-impact speed sample or explicit "at rest" flag in the provided data for either vehicle. The claim about both vehicles coming to rest on the road cannot be confirmed from the available V2 channels.

This sequence combines spatial lane/location information about Vehicle 1 and timing of contact; while V2 records a single impact at t=0 (with delta-V [−17.0 km/h](T2)), there is no positional/lane evidence for Vehicle 1’s location or path (e.g., crossing a gore) in V2’s telemetry, so the detailed sequence relative to lanes/gore is not verifiable.

result.rationale: V2’s telemetry shows a single crash pulse with longitudinal delta-V [−17.0 km/h at t=0.19 s](T2), pre-impact speed [28.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T3), brake application at [t=-0.5 s](T2), and large steering change ([220.0 → 17.5 → 22.5 deg at t=-5.0, -0.5, 0.0 s](T3)). These support a single-impact event on V2 but do not provide lane-level or other-vehicle spatial details required to verify many narrative assertions.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept
